fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Virginia Respiratory Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a respiratory therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Virginia - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: respiratory therapist license reciprocity virginia, how to transfer respiratory therapist license to virginia, respiratory therapist compact virginia, virginia respiratory therapist endorsement application

board.name: Virginia Board of Medicine — RT

Virginia Board of Medicine
Perimeter Center

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) examination

license_types.title: Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a respiratory therapist in Virginia.

steps.title: Create an Account on the DHP Online Licensing Portal
steps.description: New applicants must register for an account through the Department of Health Professions' Online Licensing Portal by providing their personal details and creating login credentials.

steps.title: Complete and Submit Online Application and Fee
steps.description: Log in to the Virginia DHP Online Licensing Portal, select the Board of Medicine and Respiratory Therapist application, fill out all required sections, and submit the non-refundable application fee ($140 for endorsement).

steps.title: Arrange for Verification of Licensure
steps.description: Request official verification of all current and initial Respiratory Therapist licenses you have ever held in any state or jurisdiction. These verifications must be sent directly from the issuing board(s) to the Virginia Board of Medicine.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts from your approved Respiratory Therapy program (Associate's degree or higher) to be sent directly from the educational institution to the Virginia Board of Medicine.

steps.title: Ensure NBRC Certification Verification
steps.description: The Virginia Board of Medicine will directly verify your Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT) credential with the National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC). Ensure your NBRC information is current and accurate.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBRC. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Virginia respiratory therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Virginia have reciprocity for respiratory therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers respiratory therapist practice in Virginia. To work in Virginia, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my respiratory therapist license to Virginia?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Virginia while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Respiratory Therapist in Virginia
hero.dek: To apply for a Respiratory Therapist license in Virginia, applicants must use the Department of Health Professions' Online Licensing Portal to submit their application and required documentation.

official_links.portal_name: Department of Health Professions' Online Licensing Portal
official_links.registration_steps: New users must register an account by clicking 'Register A New Account' on the portal login page. You will need to provide your social security number, date of birth, and follow the prompts to create your username and password.
